title: Cofense PhishMe Integration Guide
content:
## Overview

Cofense PhishMe (formerly PhishMe) is a leading phishing awareness and simulation platform that helps organizations train employees to recognize and report phishing emails. Part of the broader Cofense suite, PhishMe works alongside Cofense Triage (for reported email analysis) and Cofense Intelligence (for phishing-specific threat intel) to create a human-driven phishing defense layer. Integrating Cofense with T1 Agentics enables SOC teams to automatically process user-reported phishing emails, correlate with other security data, and orchestrate rapid response to confirmed phishing threats.

## Prerequisites

- **Cofense License**: An active Cofense PhishMe subscription, and optionally Cofense Triage and/or Cofense Intelligence
- **API Token**: Generate an API token from the Cofense Triage console under **Administration > API Tokens** (for Triage integration) or from PhishMe admin settings
- **Cofense Triage URL**: If using Cofense Triage, the base URL of your Triage instance (e.g., `https://triage.yourorg.com`)
- **T1 Agentics Access**: Administrator or Connect Manager role in T1 Agentics
- **Network Access**: Outbound HTTPS (port 443) connectivity to your Cofense instance(s)

## Configuration in T1 Agentics

1. Navigate to **Connect > Marketplace** in the T1 Agentics console
2. Search for **Cofense PhishMe** in the Email Security category or use the search bar
3. Click **Configure** on the Cofense PhishMe integration card
4. Enter your **Cofense Triage URL** (e.g., `https://triage.yourorg.com`)
5. Enter your **API Token** in the credentials field
6. Select which **Data Sources** to enable: Reported Emails, Simulation Results, Intelligence Indicators
7. Configure **Auto-Categorization** rules to map Cofense categories to T1 Agentics severity levels
8. Set **Polling Interval** for retrieving new reported emails
9. Click **Test Connection** to verify API connectivity
10. Click **Save** to activate the integration

## Available Actions

- **Reported Email Retrieval**: Pull user-reported suspicious emails from Cofense Triage for analysis and investigation
- **Report Categorization**: Update the category and status of reported emails (e.g., Phishing, Spam, Clean)
- **Indicator Extraction**: Extract IOCs (URLs, domains, hashes, sender addresses) from reported emails
- **Simulation Results**: Retrieve phishing simulation campaign results including click rates, report rates, and susceptible users
- **Threat Intelligence**: Access Cofense Intelligence feeds focused specifically on phishing threats (if licensed)
- **Reporter Metrics**: Track employee reporting behavior and response times
- **Cluster Analysis**: View clusters of similar reported emails to identify coordinated phishing campaigns
- **Automated Triage**: Leverage Cofense's automated analysis rules to pre-categorize reported emails

## Common Use Cases

- **Automated Phishing Triage**: When employees report suspicious emails through Cofense Reporter, automatically ingest them into T1 Agentics, extract IOCs, enrich against threat intelligence sources, and categorize the report as phishing, spam, or clean
- **Phishing Campaign Detection**: Use Cofense Triage's cluster analysis to identify coordinated phishing campaigns, then automatically create T1 Agentics incidents to track response across all affected users
- **Human Sensor Network**: Leverage employee-reported emails as an additional detection layer, correlating user reports with technical email security alerts for comprehensive phishing detection
- **Security Awareness Metrics**: Pull simulation results into T1 Agentics dashboards to track organizational phishing susceptibility trends and target training programs
- **IOC Operationalization**: Extract indicators from confirmed phishing emails and automatically push them to email gateways, proxies, and SIEM detection rules through T1 Agentics playbooks

## Troubleshooting

| Issue | Solution |
|-------|----------|
| **401 Unauthorized** | Verify your API token is correct and has not expired. Regenerate from the Cofense Triage admin console. |
| **No Reports Found** | Ensure employees have the Cofense Reporter plugin installed and are actively reporting. Check that the Triage instance is processing reported emails. |
| **Missing IOCs** | Cofense Triage extracts IOCs automatically, but some may require manual analysis. Check that auto-analysis rules are configured in Triage. |
| **API Version Mismatch** | Ensure T1 Agentics is configured for the correct Cofense Triage API version (v1 or v2). Check your Triage version for compatibility. |
| **Connection Timeout** | Verify network connectivity to your Cofense Triage instance. For on-premises deployments, check firewall rules and proxy settings. |
